title: Bridge Essay: Hippocampal-occipital connectivity reflects autobiographical memory deficits in

body.text: This bridge paper connects the esoteric/philosophical concept of **Imaginal** (Phase 10) with mechanistic science from PubMed/MEDLINE.
**Bridge rationale:** Links Imaginal to mechanistic neuroscience through Hippocampal-occipital, connectivity, reflects, autobiographical, memory.

**Hippocampal-occipital connectivity reflects autobiographical memory deficits in aphantasia.** — Elife. PMID: 39325034. DOI: 10.7554/eLife.94916. Aphantasia refers to reduced or absent visual imagery. While most of us can readily recall decade-old personal experiences (autobiographical memories, AM) with vivid mental images, there is adearth of information about whether the loss of visual imagery in aphantasics affects their AM retrieval. The hippocampus is thought to be acrucial hub in abrain-wide network underlying AM. One important question is whether this network, especially the connectivity of the hippocampus, is altered in aphant
